mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, consumer spending power collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment stabilizes below 7.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and falling federal funds rates signals effective monetary transmission to consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression, or positive returns fail to materialize when retail sales decline despite rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates recovering manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during liquidity crises, producing negative 20-day returns in >55% of acceleration episodes, or loses significance when credit spreads widen above 300 bps.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 500,000 units signal renewed construction activity and wealth effects that lift equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative in >65% of months where housing starts cross the 500k threshold, or the signal fails during periods of negative house price appreciation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll gains consistently coincide with negative 5-day equity returns in >60% of observations, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate erodes real yields and prompts expectations of future monetary tightening, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when inflation remains below 2.5%, or positive equity returns occur in >55% of high-inflation/stagnant-rate episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter positive GDP growth confirms economic expansion and validates corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal breaks down during financial stress episodes where GDP growth coexists with negative equity returns in >60% of cases, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance during credit crunches.
